description: Canonical file-based task queue for AI agents working in this repo.

tasks.title: Fix UTF-8 rendering for right-dock agent CLIs
acceptance: OpenCode and other right-dock agent CLIs launch with UTF-8-safe locale environment defaults.
Persistent screen-backed terminal sessions launch and attach in UTF-8 mode with a screen-256color terminal type.
Terminal font fallback starts with macOS-native mono fonts so box drawing and menu glyphs render cleanly.
Compact right-dock Code mode keeps split terminals and removes duplicate embedded layout/order chrome.
No Electron IPC signature, backend, strategy logic, storage schema, credentials, order routing, market-order, or release-promotion behavior changes are made.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded, then changes are committed and pushed.
tasks.notes: Done. PTY child envs now default unsafe or missing locale values to en_US.UTF-8, persistent screen-backed agent CLIs start and attach with UTF-8 mode plus screen-256color, xterm uses macOS-native mono fonts first, and compact right-dock Code mode keeps split terminals without duplicate embedded toolbar chrome. TypeScript evidence refs were narrowed so verification passes. No backend, strategy logic, storage schema, credentials, order routing, market-order, or release-promotion behavior changed.

tasks.title: Keep the center panel mounted while side panels collapse
acceptance: Closing or opening the left context panel does not remount WidgetPanel.
Closing or opening the right workspace dock does not remount WidgetPanel.
The center panel can resize when side panels collapse or expand, but the current route remains mounted.
Collapsed side rails render inside stable side panels instead of replacing the panel tree.
No backend, Electron IPC, route, strategy, storage schema, or trading behavior changes.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded.
tasks.notes: Done. ElectronLayout now keeps a stable three-panel tree without a collapse-state PanelGroup key; side panels collapse through react-resizable-panels refs and render rails inside their existing panels, so WidgetPanel stays mounted while the center resizes.

tasks.title: Make the BTC workbench toolbar compact and professional
acceptance: The BTC route header consumes substantially less vertical space above TradingView.
The toolbar stays understandable with compact grouped controls and tooltips.
Existing interval, edit, reset, Pine Lab, auto-sleep, focus, preset, and stream controls remain available.
No backend, Electron IPC, strategy logic, storage schema, or trading behavior changes.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded.
tasks.notes: Done. The BTC workbench header is now a single compact toolbar row with short title text, icon-first actions, compact presets, S1/S2/M stream controls, hidden horizontal scrollbar overflow, and no extra vertical wrap. No backend, Electron IPC, strategy logic, storage schema, or trading behavior changed.

tasks.title: Auto-fit BTC video workbench to available screen space
acceptance: The BTC research station grid uses available vertical screen space when the app window grows.
The row height is derived from measured container dimensions instead of staying fixed at 24px.
Focused BTC video mode expands the selected stream into unused video space without mutating the persisted custom layout.
TradingView, YouTube mute/fullscreen, performance auto-suspend, and Pine Lab behavior remain unchanged.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded.
tasks.notes: Done. The BTC workbench now measures the grid container, derives row height from available height, and expands focused video mode into the combined video area without mutating persisted custom layouts. No backend, Electron IPC, strategy logic, storage schema, or trading behavior changed.

tasks.title: Improve terminal readability with a lightweight hacker visual style
acceptance: OpenCode and other right-dock agent CLIs render high-contrast terminal text.
ANSI black and bright-black output remains readable on the matte dark terminal background.
Terminal chrome uses a lightweight hacker style without heavy blur, text shadow, or always-on animation.
Rainbow and accent effects remain opt-in and animate only in the full performance profile.
No Electron IPC, node-pty, backend, terminal persistence, or CLI command behavior changes are made.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded.
tasks.notes: Done. Shared terminal renderer now uses a high-contrast matte xterm palette with readable ANSI black/bright-black, lighter terminal chrome, static low-cost accents for daily-light and ultra-light profiles, and opt-in accent animation only for the full profile. No Electron IPC, node-pty, backend, terminal persistence, CLI command behavior, strategy logic, credentials, order routing, market orders, or release promotion changed.

tasks.title: Make Agent View drive focused right-dock consoles
acceptance: Already-open terminal instances apply the shared hacker-light theme and settings refresh without killing PTY sessions.
Agent View opens an existing session in the right dock code console with terminal layout set to focus.
Agent View attach, launch, retry, and send flows keep the right dock focused on the relevant console.
Agent View remains the control layer and does not embed duplicate terminal instances.
No Electron IPC, node-pty, backend, terminal persistence, CLI command behavior, strategy logic, credentials, order routing, or market-order behavior changes are made.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded.
tasks.notes: Done. Mounted xterm instances now re-apply the shared hacker-light theme and app terminal settings without killing PTY sessions, and Agent View attach, launch, retry, Claude View, and send flows switch the right dock to Code with terminal layout set to focus. No Electron IPC, node-pty, backend, terminal persistence schema, CLI command behavior, strategy logic, credentials, order routing, or market-order behavior changed.

tasks.title: Keep dev sessions stable while hardening Strategy Pod harness state
acceptance: `npm run dev` keeps renderer HMR as the default loop without automatically watching native Electron files for shell restarts.
Native Electron watch/restart remains available through an explicit command.
Strategy Inspector preserves operator selection state across renderer reloads.
Route dynamic-import recovery does not silently reload the renderer during development.
Harness validation fails when `progress/current.md` names an active task that does not exist in `agent_tasks.json`.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded.
tasks.notes: Done. `npm run dev` is now the stable renderer/HMR loop, native Electron watch/restart is explicit via `npm run dev:watch-native`, Strategy Inspector state survives renderer reloads, dynamic route import recovery no longer auto-reloads in development, and agent:check now fails when progress/current.md names a missing active task.

tasks.title: Implement Strategy Mission Locks for one-strategy-per-LLM sessions
acceptance: Strategy Factory reserves a strategy_id before launching a frontier CLI.
The shared CLI/API claim flow creates or updates scaffold files, agent_tasks.json, progress/current.md, and progress/strategy_claims.json.
A second active strategy claim for the same asset is blocked unless the first claim is released.
Strategy Factory prompts force the LLM to create or modify exactly the assigned strategy_id.
The harness validates strategy claims, active task alignment, and claimed strategy scope.
Current harness debt is fixed so agent:check can pass.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded.
tasks.notes: Done. Strategy Mission Locks now reserve one strategy_id before Strategy Factory or external CLI implementation, block overlapping active claims per asset, expose claim/list/release through CLI and API, and show active claim controls in Strategy Inspector. No live trading, credentials, production routing, or strategy promotion changes were made; any future live path remains blocked behind research, backtest, validation, paper evidence, risk review, operator sign-off, and a production runbook.

tasks.title: Manual factory smoke: build BTC Convex Cycle Trend against the current BTC daily champion
acceptance: A new backend-first BTC daily strategy is documented and registered as btc_convex_cycle_trend.
The official 500 USD taker-fee backtest is compared against btc_adaptive_cycle_trend's 94.39% champion benchmark.
Validation reaches ready-for-paper only if the new strategy returns more than 95.0% and passes the robust gate.
Paper candidate evidence is generated only after validation is ready-for-paper.
Any future live or production path remains blocked behind research, backtest, validation, paper evidence, risk review, and operator sign-off.
Official artifact paths, verification, risks, and next actions are recorded in a handoff.
verification: rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy btc_adaptive_cycle_trend --dataset backend/hyperliquid_gateway/data/market_data/btc_usd_daily_yahoo.json --fee-model taker --risk-fraction 0.20 --equity 500
rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy btc_convex_cycle_trend --dataset backend/hyperliquid_gateway/data/market_data/btc_usd_daily_yahoo.json --fee-model taker --risk-fraction 0.25 --equity 500
tasks.notes: Done. Manual factory smoke created BTC Convex Cycle Trend as a backend-first daily BTC strategy. Official 500 USD taker-fee backtest returned 115.78% net, ending at 1,078.89 USD, beating btc_adaptive_cycle_trend's 94.39% champion benchmark by 21.39 percentage points. Validation reached ready-for-paper, hf:paper generated a paper candidate, and doubling stability was stable. No live routing, credentials, production promotion, leverage, or non-dry-run supervisor start occurred.

tasks.title: Build BTC Adaptive Cycle Trend to beat the 500 USD paper-ready benchmark
acceptance: A new backend-first BTC daily strategy is documented and registered as btc_adaptive_cycle_trend.
The official 500 USD taker-fee backtest is compared against btc_guarded_cycle_trend's 89.53% paper-ready benchmark.
Validation reaches ready-for-paper only if the new strategy returns more than 89.53% and passes the robust gate.
Paper runtime dry-run support exists for portfolio_value=500 without live trading or non-dry-run supervisor start.
Any future live or production path remains blocked behind research, backtest, validation, paper evidence, risk review, and operator sign-off.
Official artifact paths, verification, risks, and next actions are recorded in a handoff.
verification: rtk python3 -m unittest tests.test_strategy_catalog tests.test_btc_guarded_cycle_trend tests.test_btc_adaptive_cycle_trend
rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy btc_guarded_cycle_trend --dataset backend/hyperliquid_gateway/data/market_data/btc_usd_daily_yahoo.json --fee-model taker --risk-fraction 0.10 --equity 500
rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy btc_adaptive_cycle_trend --dataset backend/hyperliquid_gateway/data/market_data/btc_usd_daily_yahoo.json --fee-model taker --risk-fraction 0.20 --equity 500
rtk npm run hf:paper -- --strategy btc_adaptive_cycle_trend --report <new-report> --validation <new-validation> when validation is ready-for-paper
rtk npm run hf:doubling:stability -- --strategy btc_adaptive_cycle_trend --report <new-report> --validation <new-validation> when validation is ready-for-paper
rtk npm run hf:paper:loop -- --strategy btc_adaptive_cycle_trend --dry-run --portfolio-value 500 --max-ticks 1 --interval-seconds 1 when validation is ready-for-paper
tasks.notes: Done. Added BTC Adaptive Cycle Trend as a backend-first daily BTC strategy. Official 500 USD taker-fee backtest returned 94.39% net, ending at 971.97 USD, beating btc_guarded_cycle_trend's paper-ready 89.53% benchmark by 4.86 percentage points. Validation reached ready-for-paper, hf:paper generated a paper candidate, doubling stability was stable, and a dry-run paper loop with portfolio_value=500 succeeded after restarting the stale local gateway. No live trading, credentials, production routing, or non-dry-run supervisor start occurred.

tasks.title: Build BTC Guarded Cycle Trend to paper candidate
acceptance: A new backend-first BTC daily strategy is documented and registered as btc_guarded_cycle_trend.
The official backtest reaches at least 50% net return after fees before paper candidate generation.
Validation reaches ready-for-paper before hf:paper is run.
Paper runtime support exists for a bounded dry-run tick without replacing the current supervisor.

No live trading, credentials, production routing, or non-dry-run supervisor start occurs; any future production path remains blocked behind research, backtest, validation, paper evidence, risk review, and operator sign-off.
verification: rtk python3 -m unittest tests.test_strategy_catalog tests.test_one_bitcoin tests.test_btc_guarded_cycle_trend
rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy btc_guarded_cycle_trend --dataset backend/hyperliquid_gateway/data/market_data/btc_usd_daily_yahoo.json --fee-model taker --risk-fraction 0.10
tasks.notes: Done. Added BTC Guarded Cycle Trend as a backend-first daily BTC strategy. Official taker-fee backtest returned 89.53% net with 48 trades, profit factor 2.93, max drawdown 8.79%, and robust gate passing. Validation reached ready-for-paper, hf:paper generated a paper candidate, doubling stability was stable, and a bounded dry-run paper loop tick succeeded without starting or replacing the non-dry-run supervisor. No live routing, credentials, production promotion, or non-dry-run paper loop start occurred.

tasks.title: Simplify harness queue and cleanup repo artifacts
acceptance: The harness queue contains only current/future actionable work and no long pile of stale review tasks.
Media artifacts and local/editor configuration are no longer tracked source-control surface.
Generated backend evidence is ignored by default, with only curated fixtures kept in git.
Graphify ignores media, local runtime evidence, local Obsidian state, and old implementation reports.
Unused direct dependencies are removed only after confirming no source imports.
Verification and a cleanup handoff are recorded.
verification: npm run graph:query -- "what are the core repo surfaces after cleanup?"
tasks.notes: Done. Simplified the queue from 29 tasks to 7 focused tasks; untracked media, local editor/vault state, generated backend evidence, and local clutter; kept curated smoke fixtures and the One Bitcoin dataset; removed unused direct dependencies; refreshed Graphify; and recorded cleanup evidence in progress/impl_repo_cleanup_harness_simplification.md. No backend API, IPC, strategy logic, renderer route, credentials, or order-routing behavior changed.

tasks.title: Make Graphify, Obsidian, and the harness useful as one agent memory system
acceptance: Graphify, Obsidian, and the file harness have clear roles for future agents.
Graphify status exposes freshness metadata without breaking existing fields.
The memory screen shows Graphify freshness next to repo graph counts and links.
Obsidian vault setup creates a pinned Agent Navigation Index without changing IPC signatures.
A handoff records verification and remaining risks.
verification: npm run graph:query -- "where should a new agent start for repo architecture work?"
tasks.notes: Done. Defined the Graphify, Obsidian, and file harness operating model; added Graphify freshness metadata to backend status and /memory; created a pinned Obsidian Agent Navigation Index on vault setup; refreshed tests; regenerated Graphify; and recorded the handoff. No trading behavior, strategy logic, IPC signatures, paper runtime, or order-routing behavior changed.

tasks.title: Confirm the canonical Hyperliquid gateway port story
acceptance: Docker, tunnel scripts, app defaults, and docs agree on local gateway and alpha-engine ports.
Any compatibility aliases are documented without changing runtime behavior.
A short handoff names remaining operator setup risks.
verification: npm run gateway:probe when gateway is running
tasks.notes: Done. Confirmed and documented the canonical ports: alpha engine VM tunnel on 18500, local Hyperliquid gateway on 18001, legacy API on 18000, and Docker host 18001 to container 18400. Runtime behavior and VITE compatibility aliases were not changed.

tasks.title: Add a short backend startup runbook
acceptance: Runbook explains local gateway, alpha-engine tunnel, health checks, and common recovery commands.
Runbook uses stable npm scripts instead of one-off commands where possible.
tasks.notes: Done. Added a concise backend startup runbook covering daily local startup, gateway restart, VM runtime data root, health checks, and recovery commands. It is an operations runbook only.

tasks.title: Document explicit validation thresholds per strategy
acceptance: Each serious strategy has visible validation thresholds or a documented reason it is research-only.
Backend registry thresholds and docs do not contradict each other.
tasks.notes: Done. Added a canonical thresholds document mirrored from the backend registry, linked it from strategy docs and readiness matrix, and kept one_bitcoin/research-only boundaries explicit. No strategy was promoted.

tasks.title: Create a market data quality checklist
acceptance: Checklist covers market snapshots, alerts, liquidations, paper signals, and paper trades.
Checklist distinguishes source-of-truth backend fields from fallback or legacy UI fields.
tasks.notes: Done. Added a data quality checklist covering market snapshots, alerts, liquidations, paper signals, and paper trades, with backend source-of-truth fields and fallback/legacy cautions.

tasks.title: Document paper-trade review criteria mapped to strategy rules
acceptance: Paper review criteria cover trigger quality, invalidation, fill quality, drift, and post-trade lesson capture.
Criteria remain paper-only and do not enable order routing.
tasks.notes: Done. Added paper-trade review criteria mapped to trigger quality, invalidation, fill quality, drift, regime fit, and lesson capture. Criteria remain paper-only and do not enable order routing.

tasks.title: Optimize agent memory, harness orientation, Graphify status, and Hedge Fund automations
acceptance: Agents have a fast command that summarizes harness, active task, memory, Graphify, Obsidian, and next reads.
Graphify has a non-mutating status command that explains freshness, dirty/stale state, counts, and recommended command.
Agent docs point to the short orientation flow: agent:brief, Graphify when fresh, curated memory, then task work.
The existing Obsidian vault has an Agent Navigation Index linked from Workspace Home.
The Hedge Fund Station nightly automation uses the improved orientation commands and one-small-patch constraint.
A weekly read-only health report automation is created for this repo.
External non-Hedge-Fund automations are not changed.
verification: npm run graph:query -- "where should a new agent start for repo architecture and memory work?"
tasks.notes: Done. Added agent:brief and graph:status, aligned agent docs around the short orientation flow, added the existing Obsidian vault Agent Navigation Index, resolved the daily plus weekly cadence decision, updated the nightly Hedge Fund automation, created weekly-hedge-fund-agent-health-report, refreshed Graphify, and preserved unrelated Electron/Gemini worktree changes.

tasks.title: Redesign Strategy Memory as an interactive graph-first explorer
acceptance: The second half of /memory is graph-first and interactive for strategies, artifacts, Obsidian notes, and learning events.
Strategy Memory keeps backend/docs/artifacts as the source of truth and Obsidian as curated memory.
The graph supports search, lens filtering, evidence filtering, Focus, Neighborhood, Labels, Physics, Fit, Reset, node selection, and path/detail actions.
Default graph scope reduces noise by focusing actionable strategies and direct evidence neighbors while keeping Memory/All available.
Audit artifacts are represented as first-class graph nodes.
The inspector gives future agents an Agent Path with next action, evidence routes, suggested hf:* commands, and missing pieces.
Documentation and handoff explain the new graph role and verification.
verification: Visual smoke on /memory for desktop and mobile graph interactions
tasks.notes: Done. Redesigned the lower /memory Strategy Memory section as an interactive graph-first explorer with local vis-network, search/lens/evidence filters, Focus/Neighborhood/Labels/Physics/Fit/Reset controls, reduced default noise, first-class audit artifacts, Agent Path guidance, docs updates, and refreshed Graphify. No strategy promotion, live execution, or backend trading logic changes were made; research, backtest, validation, paper, risk, and operator gates were explicitly not advanced.

tasks.title: Define the future live trading promotion gate package
acceptance: Document required research, backtest, validation, paper, risk, operator sign-off, monitoring, and rollback evidence.
Define what remains impossible for agents without explicit human approval.
Map promotion evidence to stable hf:* commands and backend artifacts.
tasks.notes: Blocked until the human explicitly requests production/live gate design. Live remains a future path, not an agent shortcut.

tasks.title: Finish retired content-growth cleanup and add RTK Codex instructions
acceptance: Active source, agent docs, package metadata, and current harness state no longer advertise unused content-growth code or stale external automation references.
The stale local git worktree metadata for the retired surface is pruned and the active branch has a neutral name.
RTK is configured for local Codex use through AGENTS.md and RTK.md.
Core agent orientation docs tell future agents to prefer RTK-prefixed shell commands while preserving stable hf:* commands and raw-output escape hatches.
Verification and a handoff are recorded.
verification: rtk rg -n -i "m[a]rketing|a[u]toblog|a[u]to-blogger|c[a]mpaign|l[i]nkedin|w[e]bsite-hero|m[a]rketing-ai|window\\.electronAPI\\.m[a]rketing|m[a]rketing:" electron src docs scripts package.json package-lock.json agent_tasks.json progress/current.md CHECKPOINTS.md README.md CLAUDE.md CODEX.md
tasks.notes: Done. Finished active cleanup for the retired content-growth surface, pruned stale worktree metadata, renamed the current branch to codex/rtk-repo-context-cleanup, configured RTK for local Codex with RTK.md and AGENTS.md, and updated core agent docs so future shell commands prefer RTK while preserving raw-output escape hatches. No trading behavior, backend strategy logic, credentials, or broker/order side effects changed.

tasks.title: Add Caveman output-only token discipline for agents
acceptance: Caveman is documented as output-only for user-facing agent replies.
Repo docs explicitly forbid Caveman context or memory compression, MCP shrink, global hooks, and generated artifact rewriting without human approval.
AGENTS.md includes the local Caveman instruction file and the agent brief lists it as a next read.
RTK and Caveman responsibilities are clearly separated: command-output filtering versus assistant-output brevity.
Verification and a handoff are recorded.
verification: rtk rg -n "CAVEMAN|Caveman|caveman" AGENTS.md CAVEMAN.md README.md CLAUDE.md CODEX.md docs/operations/agents scripts/agent_harness.py
tasks.notes: Done. Added Caveman as an output-only user-facing response discipline for Codex/repo agents, separate from RTK command-output filtering. No Caveman context compression, memory rewriting, MCP shrink, global hooks, credentials, backend strategy logic, or broker/order side effects changed.

tasks.title: Make the BTC station layout flexible with muted clean video embeds
acceptance: The BTC station supports drag and resize positioning through an edit mode, with layout persisted in localStorage.
The default layout keeps TradingView large, videos clean and muted, and Pine AI Lab collapsed.
Layout controls can edit, lock, reset, show or hide videos, and open Pine AI Lab.
Pine AI Lab can open as a drawer and be pinned as a movable board panel.
No backend Pine endpoint, strategy logic, paper trading, or order-routing behavior changes.
verification: Manual smoke on /btc for layout persistence, reset, muted embeds, Pine drawer, and narrow viewport
tasks.notes: Done. Replaced /btc with a persisted drag/resize workbench, muted clean YouTube embeds, video visibility controls, and Pine AI Lab as a drawer or pinned panel. Build, harness check, diff check, and renderer shell probe passed; full visual smoke remains the next manual step because Browser/Playwright automation was unavailable.

tasks.title: Polish BTC layout editing so panels feel moldable
acceptance: BTC workbench has a finer layout grid so TradingView and videos can be resized in smaller increments.
Edit mode clearly exposes drag and resize affordances without needing to fix YouTube playback errors.
Presets make video focus, TradingView focus, and balanced layouts easy to apply.
Panel controls make common sizing and hiding actions quick.
No backend Pine endpoint, strategy logic, paper trading, or order-routing behavior changes.
tasks.notes: Done. Made /btc more moldable with a v2 24-column layout grid, smaller row height, video/TV/mosaic presets, per-panel +/- sizing controls, and larger edit-mode resize handles. YouTube error 152-4 was intentionally not debugged.

tasks.title: Fix BTC YouTube streams with focused compatible watch pages
acceptance: BTC stream panels avoid the YouTube embed path that shows error 152-4.
Stream webviews still prioritize the video surface with minimal surrounding chrome.
Streams remain muted through webview audio mute and page-level video mute reinforcement.
External-open links use the compatible YouTube watch URL.
No backend Pine endpoint, strategy logic, paper trading, or order-routing behavior changes.
tasks.notes: Done. Replaced /btc stream embeds with focused YouTube watch webviews, injected video-first page styling, kept mute enforcement, and changed external open links to compatible watch URLs. Build, harness check, and diff check passed.

tasks.title: Split Strategy Memory and Repo Graph into separate load paths
acceptance: /memory loads Strategy Memory without requesting Graphify status or mounting the Graphify iframe.
/repo-graph lazy-loads Graphify status, metrics, artifact open actions, and the Graphify iframe.
Existing Graphify backend endpoints remain unchanged.
Readiness routing for strategy memory remains pointed at /memory.
Docs and handoff record the route split and verification.
verification: Browser smoke on /memory and /repo-graph when app runtime is available
tasks.notes: Done. Split Graphify out of /memory into lazy /repo-graph, preserved existing backend Graphify endpoints, kept readiness memory routing on /memory, regenerated Graphify, and verified build plus Chrome headless route smoke.

tasks.title: Create two backend-first Hyperliquid strategy candidates in parallel
acceptance: Two distinct backend strategy packages exist with deterministic logic, scoring, risk, paper, backtest, and specs.
Each strategy has a public docs/strategies spec covering edge, regimes, inputs, entry, invalidation, exit, risk, costs, validation, failure modes, and backend mapping.
Both strategies are registered for backtest and have documented validation thresholds.
Focused tests cover logic, synthetic replay, and paper-candidate behavior.
Initial backtest and validation outputs are generated or validation blockers are recorded.
No live trading, paper runtime loop, credential change, or production promotion occurs; operator sign-off remains required for any future promotion.
verification: rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy breakout_oi_confirmation --symbols BTC,SOL,HYPE --lookback-days 30 --output backend/hyperliquid_gateway/data/backtests/breakout_oi_confirmation-initial.json

rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy liquidation_pressure_flip_reversal --symbols BTC,SOL,HYPE --lookback-days 30 --output backend/hyperliquid_gateway/data/backtests/liquidation_pressure_flip_reversal-initial.json
tasks.notes: Done. Added two registered backend-first research candidates with specs, modules, tests, thresholds, initial backtests, and blocked validations. breakout_oi_confirmation generated 52 trades but lost after costs and failed return/profit/win-rate/robust gates. liquidation_pressure_flip_reversal generated only 2 losing trades and failed sample/profitability gates. Research, backtest, validation, paper review, risk controls, and operator sign-off remain mandatory gates before any future execution.

tasks.title: Repair BTC scalp strategy with fee-aware failed impulse candidate
acceptance: The stale btc_momentum_oi_swing_benchmark handoff is replaced by a concrete BTC fee-aware scalp strategy task.
The strategy has backend deterministic logic, scoring, risk, paper, backtest, and docs/spec artifacts.
Backtest output includes same-window BTC buy-and-hold benchmark fields: btc_hold_return_pct, excess_vs_btc_hold_pct, and benchmark_window.
Validation thresholds enforce a real scalp gate: sample size, positive net return, profit factor, drawdown, average trade return, and concentration checks.

Local and VM data limitations are recorded; no live trading, credentials, production promotion, or paper runtime changes occur; any future production path remains outside this task and requires research, backtest, validation, paper evidence, risk review, and operator sign-off.
verification: rtk python3 -m unittest tests.test_strategy_catalog tests.test_backtest_filters tests.test_backtest_fees_and_scalper
tasks.notes: Done. Added a registered BTC-only fee-aware failed-impulse scalp candidate with docs, deterministic backend logic/scoring/risk/paper/backtest modules, BTC hold benchmark fields, robust scalp gates, tests, and local blocked evidence. The 3-day local taker backtest returned -0.09% over 5 trades with BTC hold at -0.80% and excess vs BTC hold at +0.71%; validation blocked it for insufficient sample, negative net return, weak profit factor, and weak average net trade return. VM BTC data spans only about 10.72 days, so 30/60/90 day VM backtests were skipped as a data-quality blocker. No paper, live, or production promotion occurred; future production remains outside scope and requires research, backtest, validation, paper evidence, risk review, and operator sign-off.

tasks.title: Add multi-year BTC daily history fetch for backtesting
acceptance: Backtesting has a backend-owned BTC/USD daily history loader that can fetch and cache years of daily data from Yahoo Finance first, with a public fallback source.
The stable hf CLI can refresh/cache BTC daily history under the backend data artifact root without putting heavy data in React.
The existing One Bitcoin daily backtest can reuse the shared loader and still supports local JSON/CSV fixtures.
Docs explain that daily BTC history is valid for daily/long-horizon backtests and benchmarks, not as a substitute for intraday Hyperliquid snapshot validation.

No live trading, credentials, production promotion, or paper runtime changes occur; any future production path remains outside this task and requires research, backtest, validation, paper evidence, risk review, and operator sign-off.
tasks.notes: Done. Added shared backend BTC/USD daily history fetching/caching for long-horizon backtests, using Yahoo Finance BTC-USD first and Binance BTCUSDT daily klines as auto fallback. Added npm run hf:market-data:btc-daily, reused the loader from One Bitcoin, generated an ignored Yahoo cache with 4,257 daily rows from 2014-09-17 through 2026-05-13, and verified One Bitcoin can backtest against it. This is data/backtest infrastructure only; daily BTC history is not a replacement for intraday Hyperliquid snapshots when validating scalps. No paper, live, or production promotion occurred; future production remains outside scope and requires research, backtest, validation, paper evidence, risk review, and operator sign-off.

tasks.title: Build BTC Dual Momentum Trend to beat the 162.30% vol_atr champion
acceptance: A new backend-first BTC daily strategy is documented and registered as btc_dual_momentum_trend.
The official 500 USD taker-fee backtest is compared against btc_vol_atr_trend's 162.30% champion benchmark.
Validation reaches ready-for-paper only if the new strategy returns more than 162.30% and passes the robust gate.
Paper candidate evidence is generated only after validation is ready-for-paper.
Any future live or production path remains blocked behind research, backtest, validation, paper evidence, risk review, and operator sign-off.
Official artifact paths, verification, risks, and next actions are recorded in a handoff.
verification: rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy btc_vol_atr_trend --dataset backend/hyperliquid_gateway/data/market_data/btc_usd_daily_yahoo.json --fee-model taker --risk-fraction 0.20 --equity 500
rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy btc_dual_momentum_trend --dataset backend/hyperliquid_gateway/data/market_data/btc_usd_daily_yahoo.json --fee-model taker --risk-fraction 0.20 --equity 500
rtk npm run hf:paper -- --strategy btc_dual_momentum_trend --report <new-report> --validation <new-validation> when validation is ready-for-paper
rtk npm run hf:doubling:stability -- --strategy btc_dual_momentum_trend --report <new-report> --validation <new-validation> when validation is ready-for-paper
tasks.notes: Superseded by btc_regime_adaptive_confluence (263.78% vs 180.24%)

tasks.title: BTC Regime-Adaptive Confluence Trend — new champion (263.78%)
acceptance: Registered backend strategy package with deterministic logic, scoring, risk, paper, backtest, spec, and docs.
500 USD taker backtest returns more than champion btc_vol_dynamic_trail_trend (180.24%).

Tests pass and harness check is clean.
No live trading, credentials, or production promotion.
verification: rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy btc_regime_adaptive_confluence --dataset backend/hyperliquid_gateway/data/market_data/btc_usd_daily_yahoo.json --fee-model taker --risk-fraction 0.20 --equity 500
tasks.notes: Done. New champion: 263.78% vs old champion 180.24% (+83.54% excess). Progressive ATR trailing (tight 3.5x first 15d, then 5.5x) is the key innovation. Validation ready-for-paper, paper candidate generated. No live routing or production promotion; any future live path remains blocked behind operator sign-off.

tasks.title: BTC Multi-Timeframe Trend Ensemble (229.64%, anti-overfit)
acceptance: Registered backend strategy package with deterministic logic, scoring, risk, paper, backtest, spec, and docs.
500 USD taker backtest returns > 180%.
Profit factor > 2.5, max drawdown < 22%.
Doubling stability shows > 0% positive slices (anti-overfit).
Validation is ready-for-paper and paper candidate generated.
verification: rtk npm run hf:backtest -- --strategy btc_multiframe_trend_ensemble --equity 500 --fee-model taker
tasks.notes: Done. 229.64% return, 5.85 profit factor, 19.8% max drawdown. Anti-overfit design: 4 MA pair ensemble voting (no single pair dominates), smooth vol scaling (no step functions), structure exit via consensus collapse. 100% positive doubling stability slices. Different approach from champion (ensemble vs single MA pair).

tasks.title: Publish latest repo changes and package this Mac build
acceptance: All pending source and handoff changes are committed and pushed.
The current Electron app is packaged for this Mac.
Harness and build verification results are recorded.
The operator receives the pushed branch, commit, and local macOS artifact path.
tasks.notes: Done. Packaged this Mac build as arm64 app, DMG, and ZIP under release/1.0.0, verified checksums, and left binaries ignored from Git. The app is ad-hoc signed for local use; Developer ID signing and notarization remain required for public distribution.

tasks.title: Launch interactive agent CLIs without permission prompts and make strategy missions ticker-first
acceptance: Interactive Codex launchers use codex --yolo.
Interactive Claude launchers use claude --permission-mode bypassPermissions.
Interactive OpenCode launchers use opencode with the default DeepSeek model and --dangerously-skip-permissions.
Backend Research OS codex exec remains read-only and unchanged.
Strategy Factory and Strategy Lab prompts start from ticker/asset market reads and only ask for strategy implementation when a falsifiable thesis survives.
No IPC, backend API, credentials, trading, order routing, release-promotion, or Gemini behavior changes are made.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded.
tasks.notes: Done. Interactive Codex and Claude launch commands now include permissionless flags through the shared runtime helper. OpenCode uses opencode run --interactive with the default DeepSeek model and --dangerously-skip-permissions because this OpenCode version exposes permission bypass on the run subcommand, not the root TUI command. Raw Command Palette and Claude Agent View launches now use the helper, and Strategy Factory/Strategy Lab/default trading agent prompts now start from ticker insight before strategy work. Backend Research OS codex exec, Gemini behavior, IPC, backend APIs, credentials, and order routing were unchanged.

tasks.title: Fix OpenCode Unicode block rendering in the embedded terminal
acceptance: OpenCode block-art and Unicode TUI glyphs render with correct terminal character widths.
Terminal line height no longer separates block rows in a way that makes OpenCode letters look broken.
The terminal keeps macOS-native monospace fallbacks and readable high-contrast colors.
No agent command flags, Electron IPC, backend APIs, credentials, trading, order routing, or release-promotion behavior changes are made.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded.
tasks.notes: Done. Added xterm Unicode 11 support, activated Unicode 11 in the terminal instance, moved Menlo/Monaco ahead of SF Mono for block glyph quality, and tightened terminal line height to 1 so OpenCode block-art letters render as a coherent logo. No CLI command flags, Electron IPC, backend APIs, credentials, trading, order routing, or release-promotion behavior changed.

tasks.title: Launch OpenCode permissionless mode through the supported run subcommand
acceptance: The OpenCode shared runtime command uses opencode run --interactive when adding --dangerously-skip-permissions.
The command keeps the default DeepSeek model.
A PTY smoke test confirms OpenCode reaches the interactive prompt without unknown-option, permission-flag, or command-not-found errors.
Codex, Claude, Gemini, Electron IPC, backend APIs, credentials, trading, order routing, and release-promotion behavior are unchanged.
Verification and handoff evidence are recorded.
verification: node-pty smoke: opencode run --interactive --model opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free --dangerously-skip-permissions reaches Ask anything prompt without flag errors
tasks.notes: Done. OpenCode permissionless interactive launch now uses opencode run --interactive --model opencode/deepseek-v4-flash-free --dangerously-skip-permissions because OpenCode 1.15.3 exposes the permission bypass flag on the run subcommand. PTY smoke reached the Ask anything prompt, showed DeepSeek V4 Flash Free/OpenCode Zen, and found no flag or command errors.
